profiles.label: Fluid biomarker papers
profiles.definition: Human or other living-organism studies that collect biofluids and evaluate biomarker utility for clinically relevant or pre-clinically informative purposes.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
participant accounting for screened, collected, analyzed, quality-passed, and clinically assessed samples
blinding of biomarker measurement, handling, quality control, and analysis roles
sample timing, collection state, handling conditions, storage, batch assignment, lot bridging, and technical failures
assay validation, replicates, coefficient of variation, lower and upper limits, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery
validity and reliability of the clinically relevant assessments paired to the biomarker
statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, biofluid sample availability, and open/public availability plan

checklist.label: Study registration and analytic plan disclosure
checklist.evidence: The current manuscript pack does not yet name a registry, preregistration record, or pre-specified analytic plan.
checklist.recommendation: Prepare the Transparency, Rigor and Reproducibility Summary to state the actual registration status and whether the analytic plan was pre-specified.

checklist.label: Participant and sample accounting
checklist.evidence: The pack already names endotype context and clinically relevant readouts, but it does not yet enumerate screened, sampled, analyzed, quality-passed, and outcome-assessed participants.
checklist.recommendation: Add a CONSORT-style accounting block for screened, sampled, analyzed, QC-passed, and clinically assessed participants.

checklist.label: Biofluid sample handling, batching, and blinding
checklist.evidence: The pack records sample types and the planned readout lane, but it does not yet capture acquisition timing, handling conditions, batch assignment, freeze-thaw history, or blinded QC roles.
checklist.recommendation: Capture collection timing, subject state, handling/storage conditions, batch assignment, technical failures, and who was blinded during sample handling and quality control.

checklist.label: Assay validation and analytical performance
checklist.evidence: The biomarker panel and readout spine are defined, but assay CV, LLOQ/LLOD, batch reproducibility, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery are not yet in the manuscript pack.
checklist.recommendation: Name the assay platform, validation status, replicates, CV, quantitation limits, linearity, specificity, and lot-bridging behavior for the primary biomarker measurements.

checklist.label: Clinically relevant assessment validity
checklist.evidence: The pack ties the biomarker lane to endotypes and downstream burden, but it does not yet document the validity and reliability of the paired clinical assessments.
checklist.recommendation: State which inclusion criteria and outcome measures are established standards, emerging standards, or still provisional, and cite the validation basis.

checklist.label: Statistics, multiple comparisons, and replication
checklist.evidence: No manuscript-facing declaration yet covers statistical assumptions, multiple-comparison handling, or the status of internal/external replication.
checklist.recommendation: Add the planned statistical assumptions, multiple-comparison correction path, outlier/missing-data handling, and the current replication or external-validation status.

checklist.label: Data, code, and sample availability
checklist.evidence: The pack has a source manifest, but it does not yet state repository, DOI, code availability, or future-use permissions for biofluid samples.
checklist.recommendation: Draft the data-availability, analytic-code, and sample-availability statements expected by the JNT biomarker checklist.

profiles.label: Pathophysiological mechanistic studies
profiles.definition: Systematic, controlled in vivo or in vitro experimental manipulations intended to advance fundamental mechanistic knowledge relevant to traumatic brain or spinal cord injury.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
subject accounting for inclusion, manipulation, analysis, interpretability, deaths, and incomplete assessments
randomization method and adequacy assessment
blinding of manipulations, materials handling, analyses, and labeling
experimental-manipulation timing, state, devices, storage, batching, technical failures, and unexpected events
target engagement, dose response, cross validation, specificity controls, lesion or stimulation verification, and positive/negative controls
primary-analysis accuracy, assay characteristics, statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, sample availability, and open/public availability plan
critical_for_readiness: randomization or explicit rationale for non-randomization

checklist.label: Study registration and analytic plan disclosure
checklist.evidence: The current manuscript pack does not yet identify a preregistration record or explicit analytic-plan declaration.
checklist.recommendation: Prepare the Transparency, Rigor and Reproducibility Summary to state whether the mechanistic study was preregistered and whether the analytic plan was pre-specified.

checklist.label: Subject accounting, randomization, and blinding
checklist.evidence: The pack does not yet describe subject accounting, randomization adequacy, or who was blinded during manipulations and analyses.
checklist.recommendation: Add subject accounting plus explicit randomization and blinding language, or state clearly why those elements were not used.

checklist.label: Experimental manipulation and batching details
checklist.evidence: The current pack names the mechanistic next test (Which repetitive cohorts still look barrier-conditioned in the subacute window, and which ones are already dominated by persistent microglial activation?) and translational target (NLRP3), but it does not yet capture timing, state, devices, batching, or technical-failure details.
checklist.recommendation: Capture manipulation timing, subject state, devices or assays used, batching rules, storage conditions, and unexpected events for the lead mechanistic experiment.

checklist.label: Target engagement, specificity, and control logic
checklist.evidence: The current packet already points at NLRP3 and the next experimental move, but target engagement, specificity controls, cross-validation, and positive/negative controls are not yet explicit.
checklist.recommendation: Add target-engagement readouts, specificity controls, cross-validation, and negative/positive control language to the mechanistic plan.

checklist.label: Primary-analysis assumptions and multiple-comparison management
checklist.evidence: The manuscript pack does not yet state assumptions, outlier rules, missing-data handling, or how multiple comparisons will be controlled.
checklist.recommendation: Document statistical assumptions, independence handling, outlier and missing-data rules, and the multiple-comparison strategy for the mechanistic analyses.

checklist.label: Replication and external validation status
checklist.evidence: The pack does not yet say whether the mechanistic result has internal replication, external validation, or only a planned replication path.
checklist.recommendation: State whether replication is complete, ongoing, planned, or absent, and register that plan when possible.

checklist.label: Data, code, and sample availability
checklist.evidence: The current pack does not yet declare where data, code, or residual samples will be shared or requested.
checklist.recommendation: Draft the data-availability, analytic-code, and sample-availability statements expected by the JNT mechanistic checklist.
